programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric agrees to undergo the dangerous brain surgery proposed by neurosurgeon Max. During the procedure, Ric has visions of his dead son and lost love, who present him with a terrible choice between life and death. A pregnant Helen returns to Holby, and Dom tries to support Lofty as he faces the prospect of fatherhood(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Frank comes up with a plan for Cassie to go undercover as her double to discover who killed her, but she refuses to be involved. However, a savage rejection from Rob that leaves her feeling betrayed leads her to have a change of heart. As the investigation into Katy drags to a halt, Rob becomes fixated on tracking down two of Jonathan's old friends, convinced they are the key to solving both mysteries(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane is joined by internet celebrity Mazzi Maz, and together they are pursued across Poland by guard dogs, with only their phones to help them avoid capture. Contacting a teenager preparing for the apocalypse proves useful in increasing their survival skills, but have a harder time finding online tips on driving a tank, hotwiring a motorbike or skinning a rabbit(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: In Bristol, flat-sharing undergraduates Dylan, Ben, Roo and Louis seek advice from Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin on improving their diets. Roo is a Coeliac and Ben a vegetarian, so they shop separately and rarely cook or eat together, and expensive tastes and love of brands mean that they're blowing their student loans on pricey pesto and pasta. Plus, Chris goes to Durham to see what the difference between boxed and bottled wine actually is and dietitian Priya Tew discusses plant-based diets and the potential benefits of eating less meat(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the fourth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to create their own range of electric bikes, before hosting their own launch event to sell their bikes to some of the UK's biggest cycle retailers. As the task unfolds, half of the team head to Nottingham to develop their new bike, while the other half of the team stay in London to choose accessories and create marketing material. At their events, poor pricing strategies and forceful tactics prove problematic, and back in the boardroom, one candidate is sent home(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam wants to create his own version of a knitted body suit, as seen on celebrities such as Kylie Jenner. Meanwhile, the marketing team plan a pop-up store event in London. When assistant marketing manager Grace is sent on a last-minute shoot to Ibiza, her colleague Mel is left to deal with the event on her own(n)

programme.title: The Truth About Obesity
programme.desc: Nearly one in four people in the UK qualifies as being obese, meaning their weight is significantly affecting the state of their health. Presenter Chris Bavin seeks out the latest research into obesity and the problems that are being passed down to future generations. He finds out what it means to be obese, what it does to the body and looks at the most up-to-date solutions available. He also reveals how viewers can test themselves with a simple piece of string to find out if they have the most damaging type of fat(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: The man who took on the insurance firm that overcharged his mother for 30 years, the problem of fake or counterfeit goods being sold online and advice on recycling mobile phones(n)

programme.desc: A dog owner displays very bad behaviour when it comes to their pet pooch's insurance policy and a fraudster tries to cash in on his holiday insurance with forged document(n)

programme.desc: Jimmi gets irritated with Al for going against him, Ayesha goes on a medical drive and Valerie's pharmaceutical work takes an unexpected turn(n)

programme.desc: EJ Osborne rescues a collection of car exhaust parts, a workbench and a metal table frame, before he and two others restyle them in the hope of making a profit for the owners(n)

programme.desc: Two antiques experts are pitted against each other as they travel across the UK in search of bargains before heading to an auction to find out whose haul proves most popular with buyers(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The documentary follows detectives from the Murder Investigation Team as they hunt down the down the attackers of a 30-year-old man who was beaten up after a night out in Greenwich and died six days later. Croydon CID are on the lookout for a serial burglar who has been caught on CCTV at a number of different spots, while in Westminster, response officers arrest a woman and take her into custody. However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from South Shields, Tyne and Wear, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Melton Mowbray and its property market to the test for his buyers, who believe the Leicestershire town is ideal for them to spend £325,000 on their first home(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy(n)

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates another documentary revealing the survival techniques of the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. This film follows a young tree shrew as it darts through the rainforest of Borneo on the lookout for food. The creature's super-fast metabolism means it is always hungry, but as it searches for fruit, it must avoid fire ants, giant snakes and even a carnivorous plant. Cameras also follow a male chipmunk collecting acorns in preparation for winter - but while he's out, a bigger, more aggressive neighbour ransacks his store. Can he get the nuts back?(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: Cameras follow predators living in the Arctic, who face a particularly challenging search for food, as the environment around them constantly changes with the seasons, from a continent of ice in perpetual darkness to a scattering of islands in constant sunshine. A polar bear climbs to reach a birds' nest, while another makes holes in the ice to catch seals. Plus, a look at how Arctic foxes have adapted to catch auks in mid-flight and the hunting strategies of Arctic wolves. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to the couple who avoided elimination in Sunday night's dance-off. Plus, a look ahead to the music featured in the next show and more exclusive interviews(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Daniel Brocklebank and Hayley Tamaddon search for antiques in East Anglia, taking a trip to Great Yarmouth's Pleasure Beach and discovering a scheme to rehouse unwanted dogs(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps Welsh shepherd Ioan get a leg up the love ladder, but when he invites three ladies back home, his demanding work threatens to obstruct his shot at romance. Meanwhile, vet George and equine dentist Victoria are both in the grips of a dating conundrum, sheepdog trainer Katy leaves the remote Cumbrian fells to see if romance will blossom with ex-motorbike champ David, and Sara finds out if any of the rural romantics believe they have found everlasting love in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: The pupils must address a court for the first time, which for Will means the seemingly straightforward case of defending a drug addict failed by an uncaring system. However, his confidence is rocked by the breakdown of his relationship, and he questions if he is really cut out for a career in law. Caroline is asked to give a lecture to the female bar association, but they are less than pleased by what she has to say. Liam and Danielle have contrasting first days in court, and Ashley has a bad day all round(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Babies: Their Wonderful World
programme.desc: Paediatrician Dr Guddi Singh presents a documentary exploring the crucial changes of the first two years of a child's life, bringing together babies and scientists from around the world to take part in one of the most ambitious scientific studies about infants ever attempted. The first edition follows an experiment that looks at temperament, how tablet use affects babies, what toddlers understand about gender, and where biases come from(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the seventh round of fixtures, including New York Jets v New England Patriots(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS. This time, the volunteers embark on their second week at Royal Derby Hospital, where some participants struggle to deal with the difficult realities of life and death. Michael and Piotr work alongside the emergency team to assist an elderly patient who has fallen in her garden, while Alishba offers support to the mother of a young boy who has been brought in for an MRI(n)

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford goes in search of what foods people should be eating to live longer, and Chris Bavin investigates if when we eat is as important as what we eat(n)

programme.title: The Empty Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright reports on ideas and initiatives to address the current housing crisis using some of the 200,000 domestic properties that lie empty around the UK. He begins by investigating a house in south London that has been unoccupied for more than 10 years, while in Liverpool, he meets a team bringing hundreds of derelict properties back to life after decades of decline(n)

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Michelin-starred Paul Ainsworth takes part in the culinary challenge, putting three home cooks through their paces including a test of their butchery skills. Sheree Murphy presents(n)

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on small creatures in an urban setting. In Rio de Janeiro, marmosets use power-lines as they would tree branches to scour the streets for food, and one animal becomes separated from its group and is forced to the ground, where it confronts the dangers of the city alone. Almost 11,000 miles away in the neon metropolis of Tokyo, a rhinoceros beetle begins an extraordinary journey to find sanctuary on a rare patch of greenery, along the way facing predatory bats, eight-eyed jumping spiders, praying mantises and a sharp-eyed crow. Including Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, showing how the film was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: The challenges predators face finding and catching prey in a forest, where more than half the animal species on Earth live. However, it proves a tricky environment for predators, as vision is often blocked, escape routes are numerous, and the terrain makes travelling at speed almost impossible. The programme features footage of tigers using their surroundings to ambush deer and pine martens making tunnels in the snow, as well as examining the strategies adopted by sparrowhawks, jumping spiders, tarsiers, eagles and chimpanzees(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Croydon as chef Jonathan Jones and hospitality consultant Richard Arman use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels from Loch Gairloch to Loch Maree, along the way meeting the 'king and queen' of Islonia, matching a medieval feat of archery, diving to a wartime wreck in Loch Ewe and finding himself short-changed at a money tree(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to transform a garden room in a three-bedroom Victorian terraced house in Shrewsbury for Mike and Lisa. Robert suggests a structure that gives each of them their own separate spaces, while Laura attempts to blend the needs of the whole family with in a highly adaptable garden room(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The crime documentary reveals how a 1970s miscarriage of justice, involving three teenage boys, led to a new method of police interrogation and investigation. When the body of Maxwell Confait was found in south London, three local boys confessed to arson and murder and were consequently convicted, despite retracting their statements and protesting their innocence. However, each of their convictions were eventually overturned, which shone a light on dangerous police practices that pressured the boys to confess to a crime they did not commit(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the fourth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a violent burglary and murder in Oxford in 1931, which resulted in the conviction and execution of Henry Seymour(n)

programme.title: The Day California Burned
programme.desc: Documentary examining the devastating wildfire that swept through northern California in November 2018, causing the deaths of more than 80 people and approximately $16.5billion of damage. The film features interviews with firefighters, police officers and local residents who came face to face with the fire, including a nurse who evacuated her patients before finding herself trapped in the middle of the blaze(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of an eco-friendly entrepreneur's alternative to straws, a range of skincare products, a device designed to make bikes more visible, and a brand of food with a personal story behind it. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Top chef Jun Tanaka has his eye on the prize as he puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make the perfect red wine sauce(n)

programme.title: Animals in Love
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin presents the first of two programmes exploring animal relationships, finding out how they meet, mate and form relationships, and asking whether they can fall in love the same as humans. On her travels, she finds several animals that have discovered the secrets of successful long-lasting relationship, including the bonobo of the Congo, Africa's wild dogs and tamarins in the UK. But there are other sides to the story, like the devoted penguin couple that stayed together for 10 years, only for things to turn out not quite as they seemed. Liz also hears how a Siamaon gibbon dealt with the death of his life partner and meets a pair of elephants with an extraordinary friendship(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: Examining the hunting methods of aquatic predators. The vastness of oceans means that wildlife is scattered over a large area, and animals often have to travel great distances in search of food. Blue whales' great size proves useful in their search for krill, while frigate birds have developed a clever means of catching food without having to land on water. Also featuring footage of sharks, sea lions, spinner dolphins, albatross and sargassum fish(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Clevedon, where artist Jon Webster and globetrotting retiree Caroline Crutchley learn the tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit(n)

programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to some of the couples and looks at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill. Plus, further progress on the contestants' training with Ian Waite(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his Grand Tour with a chilly swim across Loch Ba high on Rannoch Moor and struggles against the elements while trainspotting. He also meets some veteran Tunnel Tigers - men who tunnelled deep inside the Grampians, diverting water to hydroelectric schemes(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King mount their bikes for the final leg of their epic journey along the American road. They begin in Las Vegas, where they learn how to make apparently the best mashed potato in the world - inspiring them to cook a massive lobster and prawn cocktail. Back on the road they finally reach California, where they follow the path of the 49ers during the gold rush and learn about cooking with marijuana - which is perfectly legal in Los Angeles. They also enjoy the best tacos they have ever eaten at a taco festival, before reaching Santa Monica pier - the end of their trip(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: As Kenzo searches Saburo's apartment for clues to his brother's whereabouts, he comes face to face with an assassin who gives him a message from the mob boss Mr Abbot - go home or go to the morgue. But Kenzo is reluctant to leave London without Yuto, and after news from Tokyo that the body count is rising, he realises he has to find his brother before the gang war gets out of control. Besides, he has also begun to form other, more personal, attachments in the English capital. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools presents the second the long-running music show, introducing Liam Gallagher with songs from his second solo album Why Me? Why Not, featuring his son Gene playing drums on The River. Also performing are four-piece Australian rockers Amyl and the Sniffers, Surrey singer-songwriter Rex Orange County and rapper Kojey Radical, with tracks from his latest EP Cashmere Tears. The line-up is completed by soul singer Celeste, making her Later debut, and singer and podcast queen Jessie Ware, who also co-hosts tomorrow's longer show(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The crime documentary reveals how a 1970s miscarriage of justice, involving three teenage boys, led to a new method of police interrogation and investigation. When the body of Maxwell Confait was found in south London, three local boys confessed to arson and murder and were consequently convicted, despite retracting their statements and protesting their innocence. However, each of their convictions were eventually overturned, which shone a light on dangerous police practices that pressured the boys to confess to a crime they did not commit(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow(n)

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: Cameras follow a white tailed eagle around the Isle of Mull on Scotland's west coast. She is the biggest bird around, but that does not stop her being challenged by ravens(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Reporter Darragh MacIntyre hears the inside story of the peace process from within the IRA and the British government. He discovers new information about the internal battle for control of the IRA during the most delicate days of the peace process and learns about the scale of secrecy that still surrounds the conflict(n)

programme.title: Contact
programme.desc: Director Alan Clarke's 1985 documentary-style drama based on the memoirs of AFN Clarke, an ex-paratrooper who served in Northern Ireland. A platoon of paratroopers on border patrol in South Armagh, conducting stop-and-search duties and exchanging fire with unseen assailants, experience a number of eerie encounters. Starring Sean Chapman, Steve Sweeney and Gary Hailes(n)

programme.title: Bill Bailey's Jungle Hero
programme.desc: Part two of two. The comedian continues his journey retracing the footsteps of Victorian naturalist Alfred Russel Wallace by heading to Indonesian archipelago the Moluccas, formerly known by the nickname of the Spice Islands. There Bill encounters boggle-eyed tarsiers, monkeys with Mohican hairstyles and spectacular birds of paradise on his mission to understand how Wallace devised the theory of evolution independently of Charles Darwin(n)

programme.title: Majesty and Mortar: Britain's Great Palaces
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank reflects on the period just prior to the First World War, when the redesign of Buckingham Palace and the Mall took place. He also considers the challenges faced by the nation's palaces during the interwar years and their restoration and conservation in more recent times(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy shares some important family news with his brothers, and outlines his plans for the most audacious criminal act the gang has ever undertaken. He also finds himself playing a dangerous game with Tatiana as they attempt to acquire information from each another. Meanwhile, a guilty Polly makes an ill-advised confession - with terrible consequences for her nephew. Cillian Murphy stars, with Helen McCrory, Paddy Considine and Gaite Jansen(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Russians test the Shelbys and their gang, and Tommy begins to realise he may have underestimated his new business partners, and has now been seriously outmanoeuvred. As aristocrats exiled by the Soviet Revolution, the Petrovnas have absolutely nothing to lose, and beneath their civilised veneer lies a fierce determination to regain what they have lost. However, the Birmingham mobster still has an ace up his sleeve, in the form of an old enemy now working as an ally. The question is, can he control him? Tom Hardy returns as menacing mobster Alfie Solomons, with Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Annabelle Wallis and Paul Anderson(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of Lavenham in Suffolk, which was one of the wealthiest places in England in the 16th century due to its woollen cloth industry. The rich abandoned the village after this business declined and by the 19th century it had become a place of poverty(n)

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores how castles came under threat from the cannon during the Wars of the Roses, before their whole purpose was transformed in the Tudor era when some of the buildings became palaces to impress monarchs such as Elizabeth I. However, just as they seemed to have lost their defensive function the English Civil War erupted. Sam also looks at their legacy and how they remain popular to this day(n)

programme.title: Korea: the Never Ending War
programme.desc: Retracing the history of the Cold War in Korea, that resulted in millions of deaths, brought the world to the brink of nuclear war and continues to shape history to this day(n)

programme.title: The Age of Shadows (2016)
programme.desc: In Japanese-occupied Seoul in the 1920s, the police try to infiltrate the Korean resistance as they plan an attack. Period thriller, starring Yoo Gong and Kang-ho Song. In Korean and Japanese with English subtitles(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy receives a mysterious letter on Christmas Eve 1925 and realises that the Shelbys are in danger of annihilation, despite his new-found and unprecedented legitimacy. As the enemy closes in, Tommy flees his country house and returns to the only safe place he knows - Small Heath, Birmingham, where the family must find a way to put differences aside, take up arms and fight for survival(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: As the Shelbys come to terms with the shocking events of Christmas Day, Tommy tries to unite his family and makes a decision he may come to regret, enlisting the help of tough Romany Gypsy Aberama Gold, who wants something unusual in return. Jessie Eden confronts Tommy about the workers' pay, warning him that revolution is in the air. As the situation plays out, Tommy's factory manager tells him he has one more meeting, with a mysterious businessman from Paris, but what transpires is no ordinary meeting, with no ordinary businessman(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Italians launch another attack on the Peaky Blinders and Tommy realises that the Shelbys need to evolve if they are to survive, but some of the family are reluctant to part with tradition. As the strike takes hold at the Lanchester factory, Tommy pays a personal visit to Jessie Eden, but is outmanoeuvred when she reveals something she knows about his past, while Changretta plans further ways to continue the vendetta in the most devastating way possible. Elsewhere, Luca makes direct contact with someone at the heart of the Peaky Blinders organisation(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the edition that first aired on August 25, with Big Country, Jane Wiedlin, Brother Beyond, Bomb the Bass, Womack and Womack, Status Quo, Breathe, Yazz & the Plastic Population and Tanita Tikaram Womack and Womack(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist examines the building blocks of life that have been taken from the Earth's elements, including the pure sulphur mined in the heart of a volcano in Indonesia. On a precipitous dam wall in Italy, mountain goats seek out the chemicals essential to their survival, while in a Japanese bay, Brian explains how the dazzling display of thousands of glowing squid shows how life has utilised the planet's chemistry for its own ends(n)

programme.title: The Secrets of Quantum Physics
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili investigates the important yet perplexing theory of quantum physics, beginning with Albert Einstein's work which questioned the validity of the subject in the 1930s - with his ideas largely ignored until scientist John Bell found a way to test his concerns during the 1960s. The presenter then recreates this experiment - with shocking results(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of President Duterte's bloody campaign against drug dealers and addicts in the Philippines. Told with unprecedented access to people on both sides of the war, the film is shot in the style of a fictional crime thriller with a real-life journalistic investigation into a campaign of killings(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates mankind's earliest attempts to tackle infection and reveals how microbes are used to fight against disease. The programme also examines how scientists, chemists and doctors, from Louis Pasteur to Howard Florey, each contributed to these medical breakthroughs, and there is a look at the eradication of smallpox(n)

programme.desc: One explores the Double Dungeon of Doom in search of the golden apples, doubling herself all the way up to Octoblock(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing takes his Hoppity Voosh toy to the park, but accidentally leaves it behind. Amma helps him to focus so he can retrace his steps and locate the toy(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos visit a house where a parrot lives and enjoy copying the parrot's movements. This excites the bird, causing it to squawk very loudly indeed. The table is making a noise and so is the Fruit Tea Machine and when the doors join in it becomes very noisy. The Hooter is noisier than everything else though(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes to the theatre with his friends to watch a sensory performance. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble puts on a magic show for Aunt Polly and Lord Tumble(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky's bag fills with sparkles from a tap and Home Dome overflows with Tubby Custard until Po turns the Tubby Custard Machine off. A child in Tummy Tales uses a new tap in the bathroom when she brushes her teeth before bedtime(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with toothbrushes and enjoy songs and story time together(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: The children go on their first ever school trip and visit a beautiful country park.(n)

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Mr Hammond's precious photo of a catfish is destroyed, Andy heads to Dragon's Breath Cave, in Africa, to track down a catfish and take a new picture. He meets a cheetah family and a leopard before taking the safari-mobile underwater to continue his quest. Will Andy succeed? And will he get back to Safari World before Mr Hammond notices?(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook does his best to grow a beard in time for Captain Captain's beard inspection. Lukas, Archie, Libby and Aleesha play Swashing and Pirate Cannons before taking on the Shipwreck Rummage to try and win back Gem's jewels(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee helps the Squirrels learn about all the things trees do for creatures around them(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster as he finds the answers to everything a pre-school child would want to know(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Cotton-tail gets stuck up a tree when Old Brown snatches her, and it is up to Peter, Lily and Benjamin to rescue her before a passing Mr Tod seizes her or the angry owl returns(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The adventure-seeking superheroes race to save Glitch when his kite flies too close to the Academy wind farm(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Live-action comedy sketch show for the under-sixes, starring Justin Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle makes two new doggy friends leaving Evie feeling a bit left out until Waffle reassures her. The two new dogs escape into Mrs Hobbs' garden, so Waffle has to save the day(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A special star passes over the planet that is meant to make the star roses bloom, but the flowers fail to appear(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines take their dinner on the Ninky Nonk, but a ride round the garden results in a mix-up with the food(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric agrees to undergo the dangerous brain surgery proposed by neurosurgeon Max. During the procedure, Ric has visions of his dead son and lost love, who present him with a terrible choice between life and death. A pregnant Helen returns to Holby, and Dom tries to support Lofty as he faces the prospect of fatherhood(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Frank comes up with a plan for Cassie to go undercover as her double to discover who killed her, but she refuses to be involved. However, a savage rejection from Rob that leaves her feeling betrayed leads her to have a change of heart. As the investigation into Katy drags to a halt, Rob becomes fixated on tracking down two of Jonathan's old friends, convinced they are the key to solving both mysteries(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Return of the dating show in which participants are stripped of their make-up and designer clothes and set up with several unlikely men. The first edition features Miss Wales 2016(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane is joined by internet celebrity Mazzi Maz, and together they are pursued across Poland by guard dogs, with only their phones to help them avoid capture. Contacting a teenager preparing for the apocalypse proves useful in increasing their survival skills, but have a harder time finding online tips on driving a tank, hotwiring a motorbike or skinning a rabbit(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: In Bristol, flat-sharing undergraduates Dylan, Ben, Roo and Louis seek advice from Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin on improving their diets. Roo is a Coeliac and Ben a vegetarian, so they shop separately and rarely cook or eat together, and expensive tastes and love of brands mean that they're blowing their student loans on pricey pesto and pasta. Plus, Chris goes to Durham to see what the difference between boxed and bottled wine actually is and dietitian Priya Tew discusses plant-based diets and the potential benefits of eating less meat(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the fourth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to create their own range of electric bikes, before hosting their own launch event to sell their bikes to some of the UK's biggest cycle retailers. As the task unfolds, half of the team head to Nottingham to develop their new bike, while the other half of the team stay in London to choose accessories and create marketing material. At their events, poor pricing strategies and forceful tactics prove problematic, and back in the boardroom, one candidate is sent home(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam wants to create his own version of a knitted body suit, as seen on celebrities such as Kylie Jenner. Meanwhile, the marketing team plan a pop-up store event in London. When assistant marketing manager Grace is sent on a last-minute shoot to Ibiza, her colleague Mel is left to deal with the event on her own(n)

programme.title: The Truth About Obesity
programme.desc: Nearly one in four people in the UK qualifies as being obese, meaning their weight is significantly affecting the state of their health. Presenter Chris Bavin seeks out the latest research into obesity and the problems that are being passed down to future generations. He finds out what it means to be obese, what it does to the body and looks at the most up-to-date solutions available. He also reveals how viewers can test themselves with a simple piece of string to find out if they have the most damaging type of fat(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The documentary follows detectives from the Murder Investigation Team as they hunt down the down the attackers of a 30-year-old man who was beaten up after a night out in Greenwich and died six days later. Croydon CID are on the lookout for a serial burglar who has been caught on CCTV at a number of different spots, while in Westminster, response officers arrest a woman and take her into custody. However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.title: Laid Bare
programme.desc: Helping people improve their health and fitness by pointing out the damage they are doing to themselves. First up is 35-year-old bodybuilder Darren, a father-of-two who looks like he's in great shape - he doesn't drink or smoke and he's been a dedicated bodybuilder since his teens. However, he's been taking anabolic steroids for 15 years and has already suffered side-effects like lumps on the chest and erectile dysfunction. While he can see these problems, he's increasingly worried about the damage he's done that he can't see. After seeking attention, Darren is given some truly shocking news(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from South Shields, Tyne and Wear, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anonymous tip-offs reveal an outrageous plan to cash in after a flood ruins a house and, when a missing watch turns up, it is not the good news that the claimant expected(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood presents the stories of a student who intervened to stop a thief getting away and a crook who stole a large TV, but only brought a small getaway car(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the Cullens, who run the largest travelling funfair in Ireland. The programme looks at this unique way of life and explores whether it may soon become extinct(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Reporter Darragh MacIntyre hears the inside story of the peace process from within the IRA and the British government. He discovers new information about the internal battle for control of the IRA during the most delicate days of the peace process, and learns about the scale of secrecy that still surrounds the conflict(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Frank comes up with a plan for Cassie to go undercover as her double to discover who killed her, but she refuses to be involved. However, a savage rejection from Rob that leaves her feeling betrayed leads her to have a change of heart. As the investigation into Katy drags to a halt, Rob becomes fixated on tracking down two of Jonathan's old friends, convinced they are the key to solving both mysteries(n)

programme.title: BBC Arts NI Presents
programme.desc: A profile of Oliver Jeffers, the massively successful illustrator and children's picture-book maker, whose titles, including How to Catch a Star, Lost and Found and The Incredible Book Eating Boy, have been translated into 40 languages. The film follows Jeffers as he prepares to release new book The Fate of Fausto and open several solo fine art exhibitions(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane is joined by internet celebrity Mazzi Maz, and together they are pursued across Poland by guard dogs, with only their phones to help them avoid capture. Contacting a teenager preparing for the apocalypse proves useful in increasing their survival skills, but have a harder time finding online tips on driving a tank, hotwiring a motorbike or skinning a rabbit(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Manchester and has some motorised fun with a toy car at a house in Liverpool. Plus, the team revisits a London project Lucy Alexander first saw three years previously. It didn't have a roof back then, but it's been worth waiting for the finish(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A careless cyclist is left reeling after an incident with a bus. Elsewhere, a claimant tries to con an insurer with a fraudulent claim for lost jewellery(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: In Bristol, flat-sharing undergraduates Dylan, Ben, Roo and Louis seek advice from Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin on improving their diets. Roo is a Coeliac and Ben a vegetarian, so they shop separately and rarely cook or eat together, and expensive tastes and love of brands mean that they're blowing their student loans on pricey pesto and pasta. Plus, Chris goes to Durham to see what the difference between boxed and bottled wine actually is and dietitian Priya Tew discusses plant-based diets and the potential benefits of eating less meat(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the fourth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to create their own range of electric bikes, before hosting their own launch event to sell their bikes to some of the UK's biggest cycle retailers. As the task unfolds, half of the team head to Nottingham to develop their new bike, while the other half of the team stay in London to choose accessories and create marketing material. At their events, poor pricing strategies and forceful tactics prove problematic, and back in the boardroom, one candidate is sent home(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam wants to create his own version of a knitted body suit, as seen on celebrities such as Kylie Jenner. Meanwhile, the marketing team plan a pop-up store event in London. When assistant marketing manager Grace is sent on a last-minute shoot to Ibiza, her colleague Mel is left to deal with the event on her own(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as guests including comedian Ross Noble joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Truth About Obesity
programme.desc: Nearly one in four people in the UK qualifies as being obese, meaning their weight is significantly affecting the state of their health. Presenter Chris Bavin seeks out the latest research into obesity and the problems that are being passed down to future generations. He finds out what it means to be obese, what it does to the body and looks at the most up-to-date solutions available. He also reveals how viewers can test themselves with a simple piece of string to find out if they have the most damaging type of fat(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Viv, Richard, Lizzie and Chris pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira enjoys a sense of freedom, Cain makes preparations, and Chas puts on a brave face for the pub opening day(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: The team tackles one of their most difficult plots in a garden in south Wales, helping father-of-three Chris Garlick, who lost his hands and feet to sepsis just 22 months earlier. Katie Rushworth creates a contemporary style water-feature-cum bench that would look good in any garden and Frances Tophill shows how soft naturalistic planting with the emphasis on scent can create the perfect garden for wellness(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined by music icon Iggy Pop, star of the hotly anticipated new Breaking Bad movie El Camino, Aaron Paul, comedian Jack Whitehall and Radio 1 DJ Maya Jama. Plus, BRIT Award-winning Sam Fender performs a track from his number one album(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Officer Estefania pushes passengers through security with a unique handling method, while drugs dog Cole works to protect the border(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. In the first episode, Paul meets seven staffie puppies whose mother has rejected them, before he visits a 13-year-old mongrel who was found wandering the streets of North London. Elsewhere, Paul lends a hand building a ladder and a platform for nine-year-old Archie(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel hits the bottle following Sinead's rejection, Craig turns on a devastated Bethany, and Imran tackles David about the incriminating video footage(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who is forced to retrain as a GP and takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village where he soon finds himself at odds with the locals. Martin investigates after local farmer Dennis Thorpe shoots at Robert for taking some of his eggs. Meanwhile, Emily returns to the surgery to see Martin after her acne medication caused a bad reaction, a secret bidder buys the Crab and Lobster pub, and Louisa ponders if she should see a fertility specialist(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with members of Westminster, topical guests and cultural figures. With updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key-players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from the 10th and final round of the season at Brands Hatch, where the 28th, 29th and 30th races of the campaign were set to take place. With reports from Louise Goodman and commentary by David Addison and Tim Harvey(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. On the third and final leg of his odyssey, he begins in Northern Canada before travelling into the icy wilderness of Alaska. Along the way, Alexander meets people who have headed north to discover adventure and seek their fortune, as well as digging for gold with modern day prospectors. He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Plastic is one of the most common substances ever invented, used in everything from packaging to plumbing to plastic bags. And while the environmental issues are now very clear, there is now a hidden problem. According to a recent report, people may be ingesting a credit-card amount of plastic every week, which could have serious health implications. Lucy Siegle investigates(n)

programme.desc: Faith is under pressure, Chas and Paddy agree on a name, and Lydia's suspicions are raised(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition of the series, dedicated to the worldwide search for the remains of soldiers killed during the First World War with no known graves. The programme follows the work of an elite all-female team at the Ministry of Defence known as the 'War Detectives', whose job is to locate the final resting places of 500,000 service personnel who lost their lives during the conflict(n)

He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia(n)

programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: To celebrate the cinema re-issue of Singin' in the Rain and the BFI's Musicals! season across the UK, Gene Kelly's widow Patricia Ward Kelly is in the studio to chat about Gene's legacy as well as living and working with him.(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Stalybridge, Greater Manchester, sort the treasures from their trash. Dealer Mark Stevens breaks the bank for a fabulous Rolex, it's tea for two with Jan Keyne, while Jon O'Marah fails to expect the Spanish Inquisition(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A family from Newcastle answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Josh, Steve, Orchid and Coralie pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear suggests a name for the baby, Victoria is thrown by a discovery, and a plan reaches fruition(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Plastic is one of the most common substances ever invented, used in everything from packaging to plumbing to plastic bags. And while the environmental issues are now very clear, there is now a hidden problem. According to a recent report, people may be ingesting a credit-card amount of plastic every week, which could have serious health implications. Lucy Siegle investigates(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith is under pressure, Chas and Paddy agree on a name, and Lydia's suspicions are raised(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition of the series, dedicated to the worldwide search for the remains of soldiers killed during the First World War with no known graves. The programme follows the work of an elite all-female team at the Ministry of Defence known as the 'War Detectives', whose job is to locate the final resting places of 500,000 service personnel who lost their lives during the conflict(n)

With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo)(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of eminent lawyer Anaan Kamel is found washed up by an underground river, suspicion falls on Marcus Layton, a former employee of the victim's chambers who was recently fired by her husband Raheel. Text messages suggest an affair that had progressed to blackmail, but Banks and Morton struggle to find further information about Marcus, as his records only seem to date back five years. And when his corpse is found upstream in what looks to be a contract killing, the case becomes much more complicated(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Geoff now a marked man, he and daughter Evie are put under watch at a hotel while the search for Mullen is widened. However, when the suspect follows Evie's boyfriend to the location and sets off the fire alarm, he draws Geoff and Evie outside. Meanwhile, Banks believes loan shark Hargreaves is behind the hit on Marcus, and manages to extend his stay in the cell, despite having very little evidence against him. The conclusion of the two-part case, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny and Dennis are forced to cut short their honeymoon in Spain, and Gina grows increasingly unhappy at the thought of having to give up her job as pub licensee to marry a policeman. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy hides away in his flat, and Jim and Liz show a united front to console him. Curly tells Reg he does not want Elaine to go. Raquel is thrilled to receive a job offer(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy gets fed up with all the workers gossiping about Amy. Reg realises Curly has gone above him to Head Office. Derek and Mavis decide to entry the poetry contest(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lynn lays down the law to Turner, while Pollard takes a risk. Zoe plucks up her courage, and Leonard bravely makes a decision(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the day of the Hotten show. Frank is plagued by memories. Jack and Sarah are struck with guilt. Nick and Seth decide to share their high spirits(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz Merrick faces a trying time when one of her patients misses an appointment to discuss medication, and subsequently ends up charged with drink-driving, while Vernon's pal David is kidnapped and held to ransom. Merton is delighted Jenny has agreed to marry him, and Bellamy finds out that Gina is carrying his baby. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Geoffrey Hughes and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo invite a German guest to their dinner party - but Mr Roper demonstrates that the Second World War has not ended as far as he is concerned(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old friend of Merton is sent from CID to help track down a bank robber, and he agrees to be best man at the wedding, while Jenny's disgraced brother Steve tries to patch things up with his sister before she ties the knot. programme.desc: Michael persuades Sangeeta to let her hair down, while all hell breaks loose when Amos tells Annie the truth(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg and Maureen decide to get married as soon as possible. Jim refuses to let Andy throw his life away. Elaine accuses Curly of ruining her career.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo)(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of eminent lawyer Anaan Kamel is found washed up by an underground river, suspicion falls on Marcus Layton, a former employee of the victim's chambers who was recently fired by her husband Raheel. Text messages suggest an affair that had progressed to blackmail, but Banks and Morton struggle to find further information about Marcus, as his records only seem to date back five years. And when his corpse is found upstream in what looks to be a contract killing, the case becomes much more complicated(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Geoff now a marked man, he and daughter Evie are put under watch at a hotel while the search for Mullen is widened. However, when the suspect follows Evie's boyfriend to the location and sets off the fire alarm, he draws Geoff and Evie outside. Meanwhile, Banks believes loan shark Hargreaves is behind the hit on Marcus, and manages to extend his stay in the cell, despite having very little evidence against him. The conclusion of the two-part case, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The local gossips go to town when a naked and mutilated corpse is discovered in the middle of a crop circle. However, their lurid tales of extraterrestrial abduction soon take a turn for the macabre when more similarly injured cadavers are discovered, causing DCI Barnaby to question his down-to-earth approach. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)
