programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Counterfeiters lead Simon a merry dance from London to Switzerland via Paris, and the involvement of a bogus countess only complicates matters further. Roger Moore stars, with Kate O'Mara and Philip Madoc(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A veteran officer is murdered, but is accused of corruption when he is found to be carrying $10,000. Theo has 48 hours to prove his innocence so that he can be buried with honours(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner involves himself in a controversial manslaughter case during a fishing holiday, but encounters hostility from the residents of the resort(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spots a potential darts champion and enters him in a contest, where he duly gets nobbled. Undeterred, the dodgy dealer comes up with the idea of staging his own darts tournament. However, fewer players than expected enter, and Arthur is left scrambling to raise the prize money. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is called in to catch a gunman who has stolen cash from a lock-up. Hard-hitting detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to investigate an incident dating back to 1953 after a death-bed confession reveals the key witness in a major corruption trial did not commit suicide as was believed, but was murdered by the police officers assigned to protect her. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A temperamental film actress becomes the apparent victim of an abduction from the set of her latest movie, and Simon must find out whether her disappearance is a publicity stunt or something altogether more sinister. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: After the owner of a jewellery company is mugged and murdered on the street, Kojak suspects that it is no ordinary crime. His investigation reveals that the man's company has recently been taken over by some very questionable people. Scatman Crothers (The Shining) guest stars(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: After being approached by a student at a talk taking place at a university, the coroner investigates a prison killing in which the murder weapon was a hot dog(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry's girlfriend Debbie starts work as a mobile hairdresser. Unfortunately the first house she attends is robbed, and she and Terry become suspects. When Arthur becomes involved, the police take over his lock-up. Vintage comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. As he proceeds, the case causes him to become plagued by doubt and believe he sent an innocent man to jail for the murder of his former partner. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two villains rob an art dealer of a valuable Goya painting, which they plan to sell in Amsterdam. Regan deduces the dealer's female assistant is an accomplice, and after leaning on her sets off to track down the duo. Seventies crime drama, starring John Thaw(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mathew Horne, Danny Care, Jodie Kidd and Al Murray pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: The popularity of dashcams, mobiles and cameras mounted on cyclists' helmets means that many road accidents and near-misses across Britain are now being recorded. This documentary features real-life incidents captured by people at the centre of the action, including the man who could hold the world record for the tightest parallel park, and what happened when a man's runaway tractor crashed into his ex-wife's garden(n)

programme.title: Blade (1998)
programme.desc: A half-vampire possesses all the strengths inherent in bloodsuckers with none of their weaknesses, and is determined to wipe the monsters from the face of the Earth. However, he faces a deadly new threat in a renegade vampire who is close to decoding an ancient prophecy that will allow him to repopulate the world with his kind. Action thriller, starring Wesley Snipes, Stephen Dorff and Kris Kristofferson(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Blade (1998)
programme.desc: A half-vampire possesses all the strengths inherent in bloodsuckers with none of their weaknesses, and is determined to wipe the monsters from the face of the Earth. However, he faces a deadly new threat in a renegade vampire who is close to decoding an ancient prophecy that will allow him to repopulate the world with his kind. Action thriller, starring Wesley Snipes, Stephen Dorff and Kris Kristofferson(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie is captured and held hostage in a vicarage by four German terrorists - and as time runs out, he finds himself forced to make a deal with them before anyone gets hurt. Gabrielle Drake guest stars, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spots a potential darts champion and enters him in a contest, where he duly gets nobbled. Undeterred, the dodgy dealer comes up with the idea of staging his own darts tournament. However, fewer players than expected enter, and Arthur is left scrambling to raise the prize money. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry finds himself competing in a motor rally after the team is hired to research, secretly, stolen secret Swiss bank account information to help stop a Swiss bank robbery. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase(n)

programme.desc: The secret of producing a classic Drumstick lolly at Britain's biggest family-owned sweet factory, and the making of guitars in demand from the likes of Dolly Parton and Ed Sheeran. Plus, how a roll of steel is turned into a finished radiator in a matter of minutes, and a company combining robotics and traditional artistry to create shotguns(n)

Action thriller, with Jason Statham, Amy Smart and Jose Pablo Cantillo(n)

programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley is joined by Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little for the first day of the event from the Lokhalle in Goettingen, Germany, featuring eight first-round matches, including Michael van Gerwen v Ross Smith, James Wade v Jonny Clayton and Rob Cross v Darren Webster. Wade was the victorious player in Dortmund last year, overcoming home player Martin Schindler in the first round, and defeating the likes of Gerwyn Price and Max Hopp en route to the final, where he prevailed against Simon Whitlock. With commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling(n)

They are to stay in Otjeme village, home to 100 semi-nomadic Himba cattle herders, who choose to live very traditional lives, but are overwhelmed by what their first impressions. programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: Cameras go inside the Segregation Unit at HMP Winchester, a prison within a prison where some of the most difficult and disruptive offenders are sent. When segregation was first introduced to modern prisons in the mid-19th century, its aim was to force prisoners to reflect on their bad deeds. In recent years, the punishment has come under increasing scrutiny for the detrimental effect it can have on mental health, well-being and rehabilitation. The film explores why the units still exist and how prisons strive to deal with some of their most complex individuals(n)

The properties include a pioneering home made entirely from cork in Eton, an innovative Derbyshire stone cottage, a bold family home on the South Downs with a vaulted interior, a micro-home built on the site of a double garage in London, and an extraordinary ultra-low-energy house in Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.desc: Engineer and film-maker Rob Bell presents an exploration of the global phenomenon that is the skyscraper, from the world's tallest building to those currently under construction. Rob examines the tallest of them all - the Burj Khalifa in Dubai, standing at almost 830m - where he discovers what it takes to build this high, and meets the woman responsible for the foundations of the Shard(n)

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: In the 1860s, Charles Pearson had the idea to link up some of the capital's mainline stations with a new underground railway - the birth of the so-called Tube. As well as recalling its construction, this programme also recounts the tale of a bus that played a vital part in the First World War. The B-Type - a common sight on the streets of the capital before the war - worked as troop carriers on the front line in France. Also recalled is Hannah Dadds, who in 1978 became a trailblazer for women as she stepped into the cab of a District Line train to become the first ever female Tube driver on London Transport(n)

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: Building works in the tower unearths some complete skeletons buried outside Henry VIII's chapel. Archaeologists examine the bones and attempt to uncover who these people were and how they came to be buried. Plus, the story of the tower during the Great Fire and a look at a handgun owned by Henry VIII, which provides an insight into the king's fascination with gunpowder weapons and its influence on the British war machine(n)

programme.title: The Sex Business

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk looks at the creation of the sewer system, delving into the archives to reveal how appalling sanitary conditions forced the city of Liverpool to take action. He descends underground to see the first groundbreaking sewer for himself, and hears about how the pioneering concept was built. He then heads to Stoke-on-Trent to see how the fashion for flushing toilets helped improve health and sanitation in Victorian homes. Mass production of ceramic toilets also became big business, creating many brands names still in existence today(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Crafting
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Rosie Wolfenden and four passionate hobby crafters head to the Haddon Hall Craft Fair in Bakewell, Derbyshire. Brian loves turning old furniture into new pieces, so Rosie offers tips on how to photograph and present his pieces for sale. James and Bridget have an alpaca farm and make yarn by spinning. Bridget learns about natural dyes, while James rushes to get production running with his sock-making machine. Mum-of-two Tracey is a paper crafter who makes original pieces for friends and family, but she urgently needs advice on pricing(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Berkshire events manager Rob Jones and his two sons swap homes, lives and budgets with the Robinson family, moving out of their 10-bedroom Victorian mansion for the three-bed council house in Salisbury, Wiltshire, currently occupied by Sacha and Midia and their two children. Midia is a bus driver and her husband Sacha a student with dreams of becoming an inventor of labour-saving devices, which he hopes to sell across the world. Will a week at the home of a successful entrepreneur inspire Sacha to pursue his dreams, or to reassess his business strategy?(n)

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: The documentary explores the lives of straight sex workers who have turned their heterosexual predilections upside down to perform in gay roles for money. A cammer and porn star reveal what it takes to sell sex to fellow performers of the same gender, despite being straight in their private lives(n)

Comedy drama directed by John Ford, starring John Wayne, Lee Marvin, Jack Warden, Elizabeth Allen and Cesar Romero(n)

Danny Boyle's drama based on Irvine Welsh's novel, starring Ewan McGregor, Robert Carlyle and Ewen Bremner(n)

She finds work as a secretary to a business tycoon, but he happens to be madly in love with her friend. However, before long she is wooed instead by a smitten salesman. Meanwhile, back at her boarding house, she stumbles across a shocking criminal enterprise involving the landlady. Musical comedy, with Julie Andrews, James Fox and Mary Tyler Moore(n)

Comedy Western, starring John Wayne, Maureen O'Hara, Yvonne de Carlo, Patrick Wayne and Stefanie Powers(n)

The three arrive on the planet Mongo, where they soon learn that the attacks are the responsibility of evil emperor Ming the Merciless, who is planning to destroy the Earth. Sci-fi comic-strip adventure, with Sam J Jones, Max von Sydow, Brian Blessed, Melody Anderson, Topol, Timothy Dalton, Ornella Muti, Peter Wyngarde and Mariangela Melato. Featuring music by Queen(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition heads to Milton Keynes, where first to host is 52-year old hypnotherapist Howard, who is hoping his fancy menu will go down with his guests. However, with dishes including 'red treacle tart' his visitors are unsure what to expect, and not everyone shares the thrill when Howard introduces his 'Pendulum of Truth' to the proceedings(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Louise hosts the second night in Milton Keynes, where she hopes to wow her guests with her 'fabulous' menu - including bruschetta and mousse. However, the outspoken PA receives criticism from her guests - will she be able to overcome it to secure the prize?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Management consultant David hosts the third dinner party in Milton Keynes, and his guests are impressed when he introduces some dancing Las Vegas showgirls. However, with all his focus on entertainment, David makes a major blunder with the food and serves vegetarian Helen a meat dish(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer services manager and vegetarian Helen hosts the fourth night of the competition in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, and hopes that an East meets West-themed night will win her the £1,000 prize. Hypnotherapist Howard is intrigued by some items he finds in her bedroom, while salesman Simon enjoys her entertainment(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final evening in Milton Keynes and the dinner party is thrown by salesman Simon, who believes that his night of fine dining will impress the critical guests. However, things begin to go awry when Howard embarks on a mission to throw his plans off course, and Helen discovers a collection of awards that evidence the host's competitive nature. Meanwhile, Louise finally comes clean about her dislike of David, before the votes are cast and the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is near Preston, where Simon O'Brien restores a spotlight he believes may be from a German U-boat while Henry Cole gets creative with an aircraft jump seat(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the behaviour of the animals at Chester Zoo. An outbreak of disease in the penguin colony leaves chick Munch fighting for his life. Sulawesi macaque Lisa gives birth and dad Mamassa is providing pretty useless, so older brother Beaker steps in to help with the babysitting. Andean spectacled bear Bernie is determined to woo Lima when she enters her short window of fertility, and young male tortoise Football muscles in on 71-year-old Bert's harem of females(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team face an emergency when a black Labrador is rushed in after being hit by a car. The pioneering vets also treat a cocker spaniel with a bulging disc in its spine and a basset hound that needs its leg straightening. However, the dog is also overweight, so the professor insists it be put on a diet(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Cheryl and Alex Reay, whose lovingly restored medieval cottage in Hampshire was ravaged by fire months after their insurance policy lapsed. Determined to rebuild the property, they have taken the opportunity to convert it into a stunning modern home, but with project manager Alex's lack of experience and the debt piling up, they face disaster for a second time(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. Dick also converts a stone barn into a flat for Angel's parents, getting rid of 150 years' worth of rubbish in the process, but tensions rise between the couple when he gets carried away with the trees at the front of the property(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Featuring people who have faced challenges and transformed their lives, including Nicole, who burnt her foot with caustic soda while cleaning her drains. Nicole reveals that she was born a man, and while growing up in Scarborough, she dreamed of becoming a pop star. Cameras also focus on Francis, 28, who has fallen off his bike and is suffering from severe amnesia(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the rise in violent crime across the UK and the increase in attacks on police officers. PCs Matt Ambrose and Mike Lowe are dispatched to a house in Crewe following reports of a man threatening his ex-partner with a gun, and later are called to a local sports club where a brawl is underway and they find themselves dangerously outnumbered. PC Claire Heatley arrests a male motorcyclist suspected of drink-driving, but becomes the victim of a vicious assault when the suspect headbutts her(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the famous quiz, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by guests Vic Reeves and Jo Brand. Countdown regular Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner with Adam Buxton, maths expert Rachel Riley looks after the letters and numbers, and there's a special appearance by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Mary Kills People
programme.desc: Ben has decided to forgive Mary, while Mary and Des aim to move forward with the death retreat(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the behaviour of the animals at Chester Zoo. An outbreak of disease in the penguin colony leaves chick Munch fighting for his life. Sulawesi macaque Lisa gives birth and dad Mamassa is providing pretty useless, so older brother Beaker steps in to help with the babysitting. Andean spectacled bear Bernie is determined to woo Lima when she enters her short window of fertility, and young male tortoise Football muscles in on 71-year-old Bert's harem of females(n)

programme.title: The Supervet

The pioneering vets also treat a cocker spaniel with a bulging disc in its spine and a basset hound that needs its leg straightening. However, the dog is also overweight, so the professor insists it be put on a diet(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty takes to the skies above Israel as he joins the battle to protect the bell pepper crop from the Mediterranean fruit fly, and Kate Quilton investigates premium-priced manuka honey, which is marketed for its anti-bacterial properties. Matt Tebbutt visits the UK's largest leek producer, where he's shocked by how much of the vegetable goes to waste, but amazed by a mobile factory on wheels that can process 60,000 leeks a day(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Rob and Jan Porter search for a property in Sarasota Bay on Florida's Gulf Coast. The couple have a £120,000 budget for a two-bedroom holiday home where they can escape the rainy winters of north-west England(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows Melvyn and Patricia Whitton five properties in Orlando, Florida. They have £180,000 to spend on a five-bedroom house for holidays in the sun, where they plan to invite their three daughters and eight grandchildren to join them(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Mel and Macca have nine children and Mel feels that all the space in their home at Park Orchards, a Melbourne suburb, is in the wrong place. Neale Whitaker has to re-design the house to make it open plan and add bedrooms, while Andrew Winter must pry Macca away from what he already thinks is his perfect home(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Justin and Annabelle put up their newly renovated exterior chateau shutters, while Jayne and Steve complete their second en suite bedroom(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Norwich is hosted by hotel events manager Jo-Anne, who hopes a glamorous gourmet affair will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. As the five competitors take their places at Jo-Anne's dinner table, conversation takes an eclectic turn, covering everything from the Queen, Josie Gibson and Linda Robson to leopard-print pants and hovercraft(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Down-to-earth multimedia manager Karena hosts the second night in Norwich, where she hopes to impress her guests with some slightly more refined pub-style food, despite questionable table manners and hairy fish during the evening(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former professional skier Jake hosts the third dinner party in Norwich, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a menu of fine dining and alpine classics. With the help of his mother, he decorates his house to create a winter wonderland, but the games he wants to play leave some guests feeling as if they are at a children's party(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music shop manager Barnaby takes the reins of the fourth night in Norwich, and after a fish-inspired starter and a main course of seafood linguine, his guests appear to be getting into the party spirit. However, it seems his only chance of scooping the prize money rests with his entertainment after ending up with a huge pile of leftovers - and a bleeding finger(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Norwich and the turn of business advisor Richard to host. He believes his Thai cooking will give him the edge in the competition, but has his work cut out as Jo-Anne is out to test his ego. Meanwhile, punk rocker Richard feels the spirit of rebellion and hopes to claim the cash prize for himself before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team are in Hay on Wye and Bedford, where Henry restores a bespoke workbench, and Simon ponders the possibilities of some discarded planks of wood(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Sumatran tiger Kirana faces losing its two cubs, who are fully grown and being moved to other zoos to start families of their own. Indonesian dwarf buffalo Oana, one of Chester's most sensitive animals, gives birth - only for the new mum's anxiety to threaten the calf's health(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team return to provide more cutting-edge animal care. The professor's first patient is labradoodle Mitzi, who has been hit by a car - and Noel's biggest concern is that she may have suffered nerve damage, leaving her owners fearing the worst. Options are limited for 10-month-old German pointer Hector, who is struggling to walk - he must undergo spinal surgery or face the prospect of being put to sleep, while a Leonberger with a cancerous leg undergoes an operation to remove the tumour(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Jim and Simone Fairfull, who are planning a light-filled glass pavilion projecting over a private loch. As the workers attend to hand-crafted details, the spectacular design takes its toll on Jim, who suffers from a stress-related disease exacerbated by the prolonged building work(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. And as if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family
programme.desc: Britain is ripped apart by the abdication of Edward VIII and debate rages in parliament about Republicanism, while Nazi Germany continues to be of concern. Edward makes a trip with Wallis Simpson to Germany and meets Hitler, but when war is declared, his hopes of a high-profile position in the war effort are dashed, and he is instead given governorship of the Bahamas. Now he truly is exiled(n)

programme.title: Spying on the Royals
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary about surveillance on Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson. In the wake of the abdication, the couple took a holiday in America, unaware they were being spied on by US intelligence agencies as well as British. The outbreak of the Second World War saw them move on to Lisbon, where their dealings with a known Nazi collaborator raised concerns for MI5(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary detailing the daily work of staff at St George's Hospital in south-west London continues, this time highlighting the stories of patients who received support from loved ones when life became tough. Gerald, a 66-year-old trained lifeguard and polo club official is rushed into A&E, having sustained a serious head wound after a 10-foot fall. The friends and family of 55-year-old Beverly gather at the hospital when she is admitted with a broken leg after being hit by a car near her home, and Paul, a 39-year-old father-of-three, arrives in resus with a nasty cut to his right arm. Paul complains that he is unable to move his fingers, leading staff to believe be may have significant tendon or nerve damage that could leave him permanently unable to use his hand(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the long-running quiz as Jon Richardson is joined by Stephen Mangan to take on Lee Mack and Richard Osman. Henning Wehn is in Dictionary Corner with Countdown regular Susie Dent, and Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from comedian Joe Wilkinson(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Sumatran tiger Kirana faces losing its two cubs, who are fully grown and being moved to other zoos to start families of their own. Indonesian dwarf buffalo Oana, one of Chester's most sensitive animals, gives birth - only for the new mum's anxiety to threaten the calf's health(n)

programme.title: The Supervet

Options are limited for 10-month-old German pointer Hector, who is struggling to walk - he must undergo spinal surgery or face the prospect of being put to sleep, while a Leonberger with a cancerous leg undergoes an operation to remove the tumour(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns how coconut oil is extracted in Vietnam before heading to the English seaside, where she commandeers a mobile chip van to find out which oil is the healthiest. Jimmy Doherty is in Italy to find out what goes into a supermarket jar of artichoke hearts, while Matt Tebbutt discovers whether absinthe can really lead to hallucinations and madness(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Steven Weald and Sue Upson search for a winter home north of Naples on Florida's Gulf Coast, and shows the active couple five properties that are within their £150,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and sports fishing enthusiast Ian Walls from Edinburgh has decided to expand his property portfolio by purchasing a place on Grand Bahama where he can spend a couple of months every winter. With his US-based girlfriend Lisa on hand to offer a second opinion, Sara Damergi shows them a selection of homes for sale around his £111,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Margie and Grayson live in their dated house at Jindalee in Queensland with their two daughters. It was a prize home in 1969, but now Neale Whitaker has the unenviable task of bringing the dodgy decor up to date, while Andrew Winter must find a family property closer to Brisbane, where the couple work(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to check out the new boiler for her mum's apartment - but it's bad news and mum Isobel is not happy. Justin and Annabelle must get busy to complete rooms in time for a visit from Justin's 95-year-old grandmother. Jayne and Steve battle with a giant four poster bed for the master en suite bedroom, but with a little help from some friends, manage to complete another room(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Newcastle is hosted by mother and workaholic Clare, who aims to impress her guests with an Indian-themed menu of spicy pakoras and lamb curry in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedy promoter David hosts the second night in Newcastle, where he aims to impress his guests with a fine-dining menu featuring caviar, scallops, beef fillet and oysters, in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize. However, some of the guests have reservations about the host, meaning he has his work cut out for him(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third night in Newcastle sees sophisticated marketing consultant Liz trying to impress her guests with a fancy French-themed menu. However, she spends most of her time trying to keep the peace as outspoken comedy promoter David annoys club singer John and rubs mum-of-three Clare up the wrong way with his snobbish views(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Club singer John hopes that a menu inspired by his travels around the world will help him win the £1,000 prize as he hosts the fourth night in Newcastle. He also has a musical surprise in store for his guests, but faces a challenge trying to impress David who refused to take part in the fun and games on Liz's evening. However, further rifts appear in the group when Vicky wants to discuss Geordie icons(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Newcastle and the turn of hotel administrator Vicky to entertain her guests. However, there is tension in the air and the spiritual host is challenged to keep the peace when John lets David know exactly what he thinks of him. The theme of negativity continues as mum-of-three Claire and Liz both take the comedy promoter to task over his questionable sense of humour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is revealed(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Four Sumatran orang-utans escape from their quarters, and tempting them back inside proves easier said than done, while the dominant female meerkat faces trouble from her daughter, who is launching physical attacks upon her mother. A young Asian elephant keeps the rest of the herd entertained as they adapt to the loss of two members to a virus. The keepers believe it needs a playmate its own age, but their hopes rest on the bull elephant mating with the females once more(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A champion running dog is admitted to see if Noel Fitzpatrick and the team can fix its abnormal elbow joint, and a couple have a nerve-racking wait while their Rhodesian ridgeback undergoes a high-risk operation to relieve the pressure on the nerves in its spine. Noel also treats an injured dog belonging to a friend from the gym(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to the Midlothian countryside to visit Pru and Richard Irvine, who planned to build a family home on an old industrial site. The biggest hurdles they faced were blending the property in with the landscape and working the long hours needed to manage the project. Kevin learns how this scheme fared and whether the locals' fears about an out-of-character building were proven valid(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: A visiting journalist picks at the hospital's flaws while shadowing Max. Bloom and Reynolds have a major disagreement and Kapoor confronts issues from his past(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The medics treat 60-year-old diabetic Tony, who has an infected leg wound and must be assessed to ensure it doesn't spread, and three-year-old Billy, who has swollen eyes after accidentally squirting detergent in them. Also treated is 80-year-old Ida, who has pain in her knee, and doctors are concerned when an x-ray reveals a shadow on her bone, which requires further tests to rule out cancer(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Matt LeBlanc, Rory Reid and Chris Harris return for their final run of the motoring show, before Paddy McGuinness and Andrew Flintoff take over as hosts later this year. In the first episode, Matt and Chris go to Norway to test drive the latest estate cars from Porsche and Ferrari, while Rory is joined by racing driver Sabine Schmitz for an unusual motoring challenge - to create Britain's newest mountain with the help of a tiny Suzuki. The team is joined in the studio by Westworld star James Marsden(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: There are more problems for Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz as they attempt to retrieve an iconic American sports car that is so rare they have never even heard of it(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders are in Leamington Spa, and the up-market town brings out the businessman in Heavy D who gets more than he bargained for in one auction(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders are in Alton, Hampshire, where Linda is concerned that the other bidders are taking advantage of her superior knowledge and her looks. Meanwhile, Boudicca and Heavy D form more than just a business partnership, and Barry impresses with his dainty footwork(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Louisiana, Danielle calls the shots from HQ when the guys find themselves in over their heads, and Frank strikes a side deal that leaves a permanent mark(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Police dog Jax and his handler Mat set out to catch the culprit of a spate of house burglaries in Cannock. Documentary following the work of Staffordshire police officers on the frontline as they tackle crime across Burton and Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Engineer Ralph Hosier and his two teams of car buffs build scrap cars to try in a bid to out-brake Shane Lynch's Jaguar F-Type SVR. Can their imagination and never-say-die attitude overcome millions of pounds of automotive design?(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Chaos ensues on the Saturday night shift in Stoke-on-Trent, while Rich makes a disturbing discovery in Uttoxeter. Documentary following the work of the Staffordshire Police force(n)

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Engineer Ralph Hosier leads two teams of car buffs in their bid to build scrap cars. This time, they're up against pro driver Shane Lynch's Chevrolet Camaro in a plane-pulling contest. Building scrap able to get the 40-ton monster rolling will require innovation and a super-sized imagination.(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and his team of explorers head to Greenland in the Arctic. Kayaking the world's largest fjord, they dodge giant icebergs and hungry polar bears(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters find three small cars at an affordable price - a task that involves pestering bankers, harnessing large dogs and almost getting arrested. James May meets American rally driver Ken Block, and Jeremy Clarkson tests out the Mercedes SL Black. Comedian Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Aisling Bea, Russell Brand and Noel Fielding join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an extended edition of the unconventional panel quiz, this time focusing on 'The Occult'. During the course of the show, the panellists discover how a 'Hand of Glory' can bring a person great wealth. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor David Harewood hosts an extended edition of the current affairs quiz, with comedian Josh Widdicombe and Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: An extended edition of the quiz show, in which question-master Stephen Fry muddles through the middle show of the middle series as comedians Jimmy Carr, Aisling Bea and Danny Bhoy compete alongside resident panellist Alan Davies. As usual, points are awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting, regardless of whether or not they are correct(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The competitors are lured to a wasteland for a challenge involving eggs and medical equipment. David Baddiel spray-paints his face and Jo Brand reveals her psychic superpowers. Presented by Greg Davies, with help from co-host Alex Horne(n)

programme.title: QI XL

programme.desc: Barry, Darren and Scotty get up front and personal, while Boudicca and Linda go nose to nose over an uplifting prize. Meanwhile, John gives Nat a hard time for blind bidding(n)

Plus, Jeremy grabs the keys to two variants of the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG supercar - the V8-engined Black Series with a roaring 622bhp, and the 740bhp Electric Drive, which is powered by four electric motors. programme.desc: According to legend, a vast treasure of Spanish gold lies buried in Texas. At the turn of the 20th century Dave Arnold made it his mission to find it and guided by a cryptic old map, managed to unearth three mysterious stones. Each stone bears hieroglyphic symbols that have yet to be completely deciphered(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.desc: The redevelopment of the Liverpool Street Station in London has unearthed a medieval grave site next to the infamous asylum, and the specialist historians investigate the link between the grisly discovery and the 700-year-old hospital. Tony Robinson looks into the archives to find out about the lives of patients at Bedlam, while Alex Langlands tries ancient treatments for madness first hand(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team embark on their 200th dig, examining the bed of a reservoir in a valley high up on Dartmoor in Devon. The specially drained site presents difficulties with its abundance of thick mud, but the archaeological experts uncover a network of monuments that suggests the existence of a major prehistoric site. Their efforts are further rewarded with the discovery of the first stone henge in Britain for a century(n)

programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists(n)

programme.desc: The wildlife of rural Japan's mountains, rainforests and beaches, where the climate can shift drastically from icy cold winters to tropical summers(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The effect of the rainy season on wildlife in Asia, with floods forcing animals to flee from Kaziranga National Park in north-east India in search of the safety of the hills. In Cambodia, the Mekong river swells so much that water is forced back up one of its tributaries to fill the vast Tonle Sap lake - one of the most productive freshwater fisheries on Earth. Fresh grass draws nomads and their vast herds of livestock back to their homelands, but they are stalked by hungry wolves and hyenas, which move in at night for the kill. Colin Salmon narrates(n)

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: A look at Edinburgh Castle - a symbol of Scottish might that has been besieged more than 20 times and has existed in many guises, including a military fortress, prison and palace(n)

Classic comedy first broadcast in 1979, starring John Cleese and Prunella Scales(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates one of history's most enduring mysteries as he goes on a world-wide search for the Holy Grail, visiting Glastonbury in Somerset, Wales, Valencia in Spain, and New York City to inspect four goblets that could be the ancient relic(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the secretive Rosicrucian Order, whose members included Leonardo Da Vinci and Isaac Newton(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the archaeologists excavate a site in Leicestershire, which they believe contains an Anglo-Saxon burial ground. Though their initial search for a cemetery proves fruitless, more digging leads to the experts uncovering evidence of several types of funeral, as well as a highly valuable piece of jewellery - and one member of the team volunteers to take part in a re-creation of a cremation ceremony from the period(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The archaeologists get an opportunity to dig at an army firing range in High Ham, Somerset, to investigate a set of Roman mosaics first discovered 150 years ago. Initial searches suggest the remains of Roman villas could be located on the site, and the team begins to build up a picture of the highs and lows of life in the society of the time - until the cold weather threatens to bring the excavation to a premature halt. Presented by Tony Robinson(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of pioneering modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazing engineers who designed new building techniques, took risks on untested materials and revolutionised their field. The first programme focuses on the aircraft carrier(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The other side of the monsoon, as the winds bring dry air and drought. Budgerigars swarm in the Australian desert, gathering at a precious waterhole, and although birds of prey try to ambush them, they are mesmerised by the array of bright colours on show. On the island of Borneo, a bearded pig hunts for seafood on the beach, while in the Rajasthan desert, nomads haggle over the price of camels, which can provide vital transport during this lean season. The mugger crocodiles of Sri Lanka stalk the animals taking a much-needed drink and a young leopard learning to hunt discovers there is no such thing as an easy meal. Colin Salmon narrates(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson gets to choose a site for investigation and decides to take the team to Jersey, where a German anti-aircraft battery was built during the Second World War. As the experts explore the area, they realise it contained a large and complex settlement, and discover shocking facts about the islanders' suffering during the war(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Derwentcote in Co Durham, where they explore the Industrial Revolution's impact on the region by digging through the undergrowth to search for old furnaces and forges. Their investigation is also helped by the diary of an 18th-century industrial spy, and records from the time help shed light on how the area's cottage industries gave way to the power of expanding cities such as Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How the SAS War Crimes Investigation Team tried to pinpoint the location of Gestapo officers who were wanted for a host of atrocities(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Rion-Antirion Bridge, which runs for 3kms and links the town of Rio on the Peloponnese peninsula to Antirrio on mainland Greece. Located in one of Europe's most active seismic zones, its engineers had to conquer the problems of deep water, high winds and constant land mass movement to build the colossal structure(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Kevin Norris and Kieran Chisnall move three state-of-the-art trains, fresh in from Japan. They need to transport them from the North East port of Redcar to a factory in Doncaster(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Documentary examining the plants and animals that thrive in monsoon areas, narrated by Colin Salmon. Crested black macaques take advantage of an abundance of fruit, and maleo birds use the sun to incubate their eggs on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. On Java, locals climb volcanoes to offer live sacrifices in thanks for the peaks' help in precipitating rain and fertilising plants with ash. The frequent and heavy rains in Borneo have destroyed the soils, created fierce competition for scarce nutrients, and driven evolution to produce some curious creatures. The Rajah Brooke's pitcher encourages tree shrews to relieve themselves to obtain the substances it needs, while the Bornean red leech is capable of swallowing the giant blue earthworm in one gulp(n)

programme.desc: A visit to Peles Castle in Romania, which was built in a medieval style in 1914 but is filled with modern technological innovations, including its own hydro-electric power plant(n)

Horror remake, starring Chloe Grace Moretz and Julianne Moore(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A wealthy widow and her daughter are implicated in the death of a hitman out on parole - and it's up to the squad to find out whether the pair hired the victim to kill a rich relative. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A double homicide is linked to diamonds used to fund civil war in Sierra Leone, and the detectives uncover the extent of the fighting when warring factions are found in the United States. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A bedridden Jessica accidentally overhears a murder plot over the telephone but is unable to convince anyone that the threat is real, until a millionaire is found dead in his home(n)

Award-winning police drama, guest starring Gene Silvers, and Elisabeth Rohm(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate a state senator suspected of involvement in the disappearance of one of his aides. programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: After witnessing a kidnapping, Rollins discovers the abductee was a 19-year-old woman involved in the world of body modification. Crime drama, starring Kelli Giddish and Mariska Hargitay(n)

Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette(n)

The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. programme.title: Keeping Britain Safe 24/7
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Matt Allwright profile the everyday heroes who battle the challenges that arise with each passing season. In autumn they ride along with the crews of Cleveland Fire Brigade on Bonfire Night - their busiest time of the year - and meet the railway workers that keep the country moving in the face of those dreaded leaves on the line. Also, as the national birth rate rockets, cameras follow the midwives of St Thomas' Hospital, central London, as they face an autumnal baby boom(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The comedian takes over the science programme in a special episode for BBC's sitcom season, turning it into a chat show with eminent scientists as guests. He talks to them about the nature of laughter, with the studio audiences serving as guinea pigs in his attempt to get to the bottom of what laughter is, what it has to do with comedy, and why human beings find it a pleasurable sensation. The programme reveals that laughter predates the human species and may have played a vital role in our evolution(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite more famous faces to take part in the general knowledge quiz. This time, stars from the world of soaps - both past and present - are challenged to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Former EastEnders Joe Swash (Micky Miller) and John Altman (Nick Cotton) join forces, veteran Hollyoaks actor Nick Pickard (Tony Hutchinson) teams up with old co-star Ali Bastian (Becca Dean), Waterloo Road secretary Victoria Bush (Sonya Donnegan) partners former student Zaraah Abrahams (Michaela White) and Holby City nurse Tina Hobley (Chrissie Williams) reunites with her on-screen mother Sharon Maughan (Tricia Williams)(n)
